item.ptxt: Thoroughly clean transaxle exterior before overhauling it.
Keep working table, tools and hands clean while overhauling.
Use special care to handle aluminum parts so as not to damage them.
Do not expose removed parts to dust. Keep them always clean.

s1.ptxt: Drain ATF before overhauling.
Remove torque converter (1).

attention2.ptxt: Remove torque converter as straight as possible. Leaning it may cause to damage oil seal lip.

s1.ptxt: Remove harness bracket with select cable clamp.
Remove engine hook (1).
Remove transmission range sensor.
Remove output shaft speed sensor (2) and input shaft speed sensor (1).
Remove fluid cooler pipe (1) and fluid cooler pipe unions (2).
Remove fluid level gauge (3) and fluid filler tube (4).
Remove A/T oil pan, oil pan gasket and oil strainer assembly.

attention3.ptxt: For removal of A/T oil pan, do not turn transaxle over as this will contaminate valve body with foreign materials in bottom of A/T oil pan.

s1.ptxt: Remove valve body assembly.
Remove solenoid connector.
Remove governor gasket No.1 (1).
Remove cooler check valve (1) and spring (2).
Remove accumulator pistons and springs.

To remove C2 (1), C1 (2) and B1 (3) accumulator pistons and springs, position rag on pistons to catch each piston. To remove pistons, force low-pressure compressed air (100 kPa, 1.0 kgf/cm2, 14.5 psi, max) into hole (4) as shown in figure, and pop each piston into rag.

attention3.ptxt: Do not push accumulator pistons with fingers or anything before removing them. Pushing them may cause compressed fluid in accumulator to spew out of hole and get to your face and clothes.

s1.ptxt: Remove fluid pressure check hole bolt (1).
Remove torque converter housing bolts (2).
Remove torque converter housing (1) while tapping around it lightly with plastic hammer.
Remove breather hose (1) and breather union (2).
Remove oil pump bolts (1) then remove oil pump assembly (2).
Remove direct clutch assembly (1).
Remove input shaft front thrust bearing (1).

attention3.ptxt: If input shaft front thrust bearing is not found, it may have been taken out with oil pump assembly.

s1.ptxt: Remove input shaft rear thrust bearing (1) and thrust bearing race (2).

attention3.ptxt: If input shaft rear thrust bearing is not found, it may have been taken out with direct clutch assembly.

s1.ptxt: Remove direct clutch hub (1).
Remove differential assembly (1) and countershaft assembly (2).
Remove governor gasket No.2 (1).
Remove bolt (1) then remove fluid reservoir left plate (2).
Turn over transaxle and remove bolts (1) then remove rear cover assembly (2).
Remove reverse clutch drum thrust bearing (1).

attention3.ptxt: If reverse clutch drum thrust bearing is not found, it may have been taken out with rear cover assembly.

s1.ptxt: Remove 2nd brake gaskets (1).
Remove forward and reverse clutch assembly (1).
Remove intermediate shaft thrust bearing front race (1), thrust bearing (2) and rear race (3) from forward and reverse clutch assembly (4).

attention3.ptxt: If intermediate shaft thrust bearing and/or races are not found on forward and reverse clutch assembly, they may have been left in transaxle.

s1.ptxt: Remove O/D and 2nd coast brake rear plate (1), discs (2), separator plate (3) and retaining plate (4).
Remove forward clutch hub (1).
Remove rear sun gear thrust bearing (1) from forward clutch hub (2).

attention3.ptxt: If rear sun gear thrust bearing is not found on forward clutch hub, it may have been left in transaxle.

s1.ptxt: Remove rear sun gear thrust bearing race (1).
Remove rear planetary sun gear subassembly (1) and one-way clutch No.1 assembly (2).
Remove planetary gear thrust bearing (1).

attention3.ptxt: If planetary gear thrust bearing is not found on one-way clutch No.1 assembly, it may have been left in transaxle.

s1.ptxt: Remove one-way clutch No.1 assembly (2) from rear planetary sun gear subassembly (3).
Remove planetary carrier thrust washer (1).
Remove O/D and 2nd coast brake retaining plate snap ring (1).
Remove front planetary sun gear (1).
Remove front sun gear thrust bearing race (2) from front planetary sun gear (1).
Using special tool and hydraulic press, remove 2nd brake piston snap ring (1).

attention2.ptxt: Do not push in 2nd brake piston assembly more than 0.4 mm (0.02 in.).
Excessive compression may cause damage to piston assembly, return spring, plates and/or discs.

s1.ptxt: Remove 2nd brake piston assembly (1).
Remove 2nd brake return spring subassembly (2).
Remove 2nd brake separator plates (1), discs (2) and retaining plate (3).
Remove brake drum gasket (1).
Remove one-way clutch outer race retainer (1).
Check one-way clutch No.2 as follows.

item.ptxt: Ensure planetary carrier (1) rotates only in counterclockwise direction [a], never in clockwise direction [b].
If planetary carrier rotates both ways or does not rotate either way, one-way clutch No.2 assembly will need to be replaced with new one-way clutch No.2 assembly.

s1.ptxt: Remove one-way clutch No.2 assembly (1).
Remove planetary gear assembly (1).
Remove snap ring while 1st and reverse brake piston return springs are compressed using special tool and hydraulic press.

attention2.ptxt: Do not push in 1st and reverse brake return spring subassembly more than 0.8 mm (0.03 in.).
Excessive compression may cause damage to return spring subassembly, discs, plates and/or piston.

s1.ptxt: Remove 1st and reverse brake retaining plate, discs and separator plates.
Remove 1st and reverse brake return spring subassembly (1).
Turn over transaxle and uncaulk reduction drive gear nut (1).
Secure reduction drive gear (1) with parking lock pawl, then remove reduction drive gear nut.

item.ptxt: It is recommended that this operation should be carried out on rubber mat to prevent damaging transaxle case.

s1.ptxt: Using special tools and hydraulic press, remove planetary ring gear subassembly (1).

attention2.ptxt: Do not reuse planetary ring gear subassembly. Otherwise it may cause damage to planetary gear unit and/or reduction gears.

s1.ptxt: Remove parking lock pawl shaft, then remove spring (2) and parking lock pawl (1).
Screwing in bolts of 30 mm (1.2 in.) in length (1), remove reduction drive gear (2).

attention2.ptxt: Screw 3 bolts into reduction drive gear uniformly, or reduction drive gear, bearing and transaxle case may be damaged.

spec.title: Reduction drive gear screwing bolt length

s1.ptxt: Blowing compressed air from oil hole (1) of oil pump, remove 1st and reverse brake piston (2).
Remove bolts (1) then remove parking lock pawl bracket (2).
With a flat-bladed screwdriver, break manual valve lever spacer (1) and remove it.
Using spring pin remover with 3 mm (0.1 in.) in diameter and hammer, remove manual valve lever pin (1).
Remove manual shift shaft (2).
Remove parking lock pawl rod (2) from manual valve lever (1).
Remove manual detent spring (1).
Remove manual shift shaft oil seal (1).
